text: Ama, mini, m suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, má am tan ndiɛ mmɔɔ fieo. Má am tan aŋgoro, dama Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n woo­biri lɛɛ.

ref: The earth is God's footstool, so don't swear by the earth. Jerusalem is the city of the great king, so don't swear by it.
text: Má a tan asiɛŋgu gusu, dama i ti Nyɛmɛ jaa gbagbaa jinaa­biri wɔ. Má a tan Yɛrusalem, dama i ti ya Micɛra Fɛmɛ n dɛɛ miɛ kpiri wɔ.

ref: You are the salt for everyone on earth. But if salt no longer tastes like salt, how can it make food salty? All it is good for is to be thrown out and walked on.
text: Na Yesu ase ka, “Ambɛrɛ, m susu­fɔm ni, am ti ka njin wo durunya n nu a. Ama, nzɛn njin fiɛfiɛ nya ba saari wieeri, i koro yo má i fiɛfiɛ kun. Nyini saŋga ni, wɔ ka ŋgbɛɛn lɛɛ, ŋka bu fá butu wɔ, na minɛm tiɛntiɛn saŋga i ni fa.

ref: and no one lights a lamp and puts it under a clay pot. Instead, it is placed on a lampstand, where it can give light to everyone in the house.
text: Sɔnɔ be nya so má fitana su na i fa gaŋga butu su, ama, i fa taka deke su aŋgoro wɔ na i ta wein ma awuru n nu­fɔm kɛrɛ.

ref: Don't suppose I came to do away with the Law and the Prophets. I did not come to do away with them, but to give them their full meaning.
text: Na Yesu ase ka, “Fa kɔ Moyisi nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n ni Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm dɛɛ keree­wa n su, má am sunsun ka ma ba ka m ba cɛ bu se wɔ. Ai, ma ba má ka m ba cɛ bu se wɔ, ama, ma ba ka m ma i kɛrɛ bá yo yii­wa a.

ref: You know our ancestors were told, “Do not murder” and “A murderer must be brought to trial.”
text: Na Yesu akaaki ase ka, “Am ati ka bu seeri ya nam ka, ‘Má a kun sɔnɔ,’ na ‘Nzɛn wɔrɔ kɛrɛ bo a kuunri sɔnɔ, bu dí u jɔrɛ.’

ref: But I promise you if you are angry with someone, you will have to stand trial. If you call someone a fool, you will be taken to court. And if you say that someone is worthless, you will be in danger of the fires of hell.
text: Ama, m suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, wɔrɔ kɛrɛ bo a faari yaa u beŋgu ŋu, wɔ yo daka ka bu di u jɔrɛ. Sasu bekun, wɔrɔ kɛrɛ bo a seeri u beŋgu ka i ti sɔnɔ ŋgbɛɛn, wɔ yo daka ka bu fa u jina Yuda­fɔm jɔrɛ­di­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n nyunu na bu di u jɔrɛ. Na nzɛn wɔrɔ kɛrɛ bo a seeri u beŋgu ka i ti sinnzin, Nyɛmɛ fá u wura cɛɛma sin n nu.

ref: leave your gift there in front of the altar. Make peace with that person, then come back and offer your gift to God.
text: yaki u aceeri n yoo­wa yoo­biri n ka, na a kɔ na am kɔ yo am afiɛn dɔŋgu, na a nya ba fa u aceeri n ma Nyɛmɛ.

ref: Before you are dragged into court, make friends with the person who has accused you of doing wrong. If you don't, you will be handed over to the judge and then to the officer who will put you in jail.
text: Nzɛn u ni be la jɔrɛ, na nzɛn i koro ka i fa u kɔ jɔrɛ dii­biri, jɔkɔti tara i mi n jaa, na má jɔrɛ n ju jɔrɛ dii­biri n ni, má nyini, i mi ni fá u kɔ wura jɔrɛ­di­fɔ n saa nu, na yiri gusu fá u ma dan­saraka niɛ­fɔ ni, na yiri gusu nyá nyi u.

ref: You know our ancestors were told, “Don't use the Lord's name to make a promise unless you are going to keep it.”
text: Na Yesu akaaki ase ka, “Am akaaki ati ka ba se ya nam ka, ‘Má a tan ndiɛ na a kaaki wɔrɔ u nɔɔ, ama, ma u sui wo ndiɛ bo a tan i ama Micɛra n su, na a yo i.’

ref: But I tell you not to try to get even with a person who has done something to you. When someone slaps your right cheek, turn and let that person slap your other cheek.
text: Ama, mini, m suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, nzɛn sɔnɔ yoori u satiɛ, má a wɔrɔ satiɛ ma i mi. Haari nzɛn be fiinri u nyɔɔnu, kaaki bue nyiŋga n gusu ma i na i fin buka su.

ref: If a soldier forces you to carry his pack one kilometer, carry it two kilometers.
text: Nzɛn faŋga­fɔ be miɛɛri u ka a sɔ i sɔɔri kɔ ju ajaari natii­wa akpii kun ma i, kaba fa i kɔ ju akpii nnyɔ.

ref: When people ask you for something, give it to them. When they want to borrow money, lend it to them. Love (Luke
text: Sɔnɔ bo i sereeri u deke be, má a kpiɛ i, na sɔnɔ bo i ŋa a fiɛ i deke be, má a kete.” Kabo Yesu kereeri ka ya koro minɛm bo bu kpɔ ya ni

ref: But I tell you to love your enemies and pray for anyone who mistreats you.
text: Ama, mini, m suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, am koro am kpɔ­fɔm, na am sere Nyɛmɛ ma bo bu kere am wuwuruwa ni,

ref: Then you will be acting like your Father in heaven. He makes the sun rise on both good and bad people. And he sends rain for the ones who do right and for the ones who do wrong.
text: adena am yó am Si Nyɛmɛ bo i wo aŋgoro ni nbaam. Dama yiri ma wiɛ fite ma satiɛ­yo­fɔm, ni sakpa­yo­fɔm kɛrɛ a, na i kaaki ma nzue tɔ ma bo bu yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ ni, ni bo bu yo má sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ n kɛrɛ.

ref: If you love only those people who love you, will God reward you for this? Even tax collectors love their friends.
text: Nzɛn am nya koroori minɛm bo bu koro am ŋgumi ni, nzu ŋkatɔ, ye am nya i a? Dama haari lampo­de­fɔm bo bu sisi minɛm ni mmɔɔ yo sɔ ɔ.

ref: When you do good deeds, don't try to show off. If you do, you won't get a reward from your Father in heaven.
text: Na Yesu ase bu ka, “Am si am ŋu sa, na má am yi am nyɛmɛsu sa­yoo­wa fite fofoe nu, adena minɛm ŋu i na bu kansi am. Nzɛn am yoori sɔ, am nya má ŋkatɔ be, am Si Nyɛmɛ bo i wo aŋgoro n dɔ.

ref: When you give to the poor, don't let anyone know about it.
text: Ama, nzɛn a ŋa a ce yaari­fɔm aceeri, má a ma be ŋu i fieo, na bu si sa bo a suyo i ni,

ref: When you pray, don't be like those show-offs who love to stand up and pray in the synagogues and on the street corners. They do this just to look good. I can assure you that they already have their reward.
text: Na Yesu akaaki ase ka, “Nzɛn saŋga bo am ba susere Nyɛmɛ, má am yo kabo burukɔɔ­nu­fɔm yo ni, dama bɛrɛ koro ka bu sujina bu tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔm n nu, ni deke bo atiŋgberem jaja nu ni, na bu sere Nyɛmɛ adena minɛm ŋu bu. Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am n a, bu ŋkatɔ awieeri lɛɛ, ye ba du mɔ anya i n a.

ref: When you pray, go into a room alone and close the door. Pray to your Father in private. He knows what is done in private and will reward you.
text: Ama, saŋga bo a ba susere Nyɛmɛ, wura u sɔrɔ na a nyi u anɔ, na a sere u Si Nyɛmɛ bo be ŋu má i ni, na u Si Nyɛmɛ bo i ŋu sa kɛrɛ bo a yo i asiri nu ni, nyá tɔ u kaari.

ref: When you pray, don't talk on and on as people do who don't know God. They think God likes to hear long prayers.
text: Na saŋga bo am ba susere Nyɛmɛ, má am yo naŋmiɛ kabo bo bu su má Nyɛmɛ n yo ni, dama bu sunsun ka bu jɔjɔɔri beberebe n ye Nyɛmɛ de bu sere a.

ref: Don't be like them. Your Father knows what you need even before you ask.
text: Má ambɛrɛ yo ka bɛrɛ, dama am Si Nyɛmɛ si am sa koroo­wa kɛrɛ n mmɔɔ ka, ye am nya sere i a.

ref: You should pray like this: Our Father in heaven, help us to honor your name.
text: Nyini ti, kabo i ti ye ni ka am sere Nyɛmɛ yaa. ‘Ya Si, bo a wo aŋgoro ni, ma minɛm bu u duma casi ni.

ref: Come and set up your kingdom, so that everyone on earth will obey you, as you are obeyed in heaven.
text: Ma u fɛmɛya n bra. Ma u koroo­wa yo asiɛŋgu fɛn, ka alecɛɛma nu.

ref: If you forgive others for the wrongs they do to you, your Father in heaven will forgive you.
text: Dama nzɛn am fa minɛm ŋgasi ce bu, am Si Nyɛmɛ bo i wo aŋgoro n gusu fá am dɛɛ ce am.

ref: But if you don't forgive others, your Father will not forgive your sins. Worshiping God by Going without Eating
text: Ama, nzɛn am afa má minɛm ŋgasi ace má bu, am Si Nyɛmɛ bo i wo aŋgoro n gusu fá má am dɛɛ ce má am.” Kabo Yesu kereeri ya ka ya cici ya nɔɔ ni

ref: Instead, comb your hair and wash your face.
text: Ama, nzɛn a ba sucici u nɔɔ, yo u ŋu nyankandi,

ref: But more than anything else, put God's work first and do what he wants. Then the other things will be yours as well.
text: Nyini ti, am du mɔ kpini Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n ni sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ n busu ka, na i nyá ba fa nyini dekem n kɛrɛ buka am su.

ref: Don't worry about tomorrow. It will take care of itself. You have enough to worry about today.
text: Nyini ti, má am koko fa kɔ ahunma su, ahunma mmɔɔ kpíni i dɛɛ. Dama nyuma dɛɛ sunsunni mmɔɔ asɛ su.”

ref: Don't store up treasures on earth! Moths and rust can destroy them, and thieves can break in and steal them.
text: Na Yesu akaaki ase ka, “Má am tiɛn ajɛkɛ nɔɔ se durunya n nu fɛn, bo watarawam muka i na i kpɔrɔ, na i tɔ ŋgambulaari, na awiefɔm gusu bá wura wɔ i.

ref: Instead, store up your treasures in heaven, where moths and rust cannot destroy them, and thieves cannot break in and steal them.
text: Ama, am tiɛn ajɛkɛ nɔɔ se Nyɛmɛ dika n nu, bo watarawam koro muka má i, na i koro tɔ má ŋgambulaari, na awiefɔm gusu koro wura wɔ má i.

ref: Your heart will always be where your treasure is. Light (Luke
text: Dama deke bo u ajɛkɛ wo ni, u ahore­mbaa kɛrɛ gusu wó dɔ ɔ.” Kabo Yesu kereeri anyumbaa kpa jɔrɛ ni

ref: But when your eyes are bad, everything is dark. If the light inside you is dark, you surely are in the dark. Money (Luke
text: Ama, nzɛn u nyu­mbaa nya ti má kpa, u ŋuniɛ kɛrɛ wo awosin nu wɔ lɛɛ. Nyini ti, nzɛn wein wo má u dɔ, nyini n la ka, a wo awosin bambaka bo i la má aŋusu nu lɛɛ.” Kabo Yesu ŋa ya daafi Nyɛmɛ ŋu barasu ni

ref: You cannot be the slave of two masters! You will like one more than the other or be more loyal to one than the other. You cannot serve both God and money. Worry (Luke
text: Na Yesu akaaki ase ka, “Sɔnɔ kun koro saŋga su má mibiɛm nnyɔ. Nzɛn i ŋa i yó sɔ, i kpɔ́ kun wɔ, na i kóro nyiŋga ni, wara i bóro i nyi su kun n wɔ, na i bú nyiŋga ni ŋgbɛɛn. Am koro saŋga su má Nyɛmɛ ni ajɛkɛ.”

ref: Don't worry and ask yourselves, “Will we have anything to eat? Will we have anything to drink? Will we have any clothes to wear?”
text: Nyini ti, má am tana koko se ka, ‘Ye ya dí nzu wɔ?’ wara ‘Ya nú nzu wɔ?’ wara ‘Ya wúra nzu wɔ?’

ref: Look at the birds in the sky! They don't plant or harvest. They don't even store grain in barns. Yet your Father in heaven takes care of them. Aren't you worth much more than birds?
text: Am niɛ anumam, haari ni bo bu dɔ má ni, na bu kaaki kpiɛ má ni, bu tambu gɔ má bondo nu gusu, se am Si Nyɛmɛ ma bu diire ye bu di a. Ambɛrɛ la anyunuyi tara anumam, má sɔ?

ref: Why worry about clothes? Look how the wild flowers grow. They don't work hard to make their clothes.
text: Ye nzu ti ye am ma suturam sa miɛ am a? Am niɛ kabo wuram bo pum barasu barasu a. Bu yo má juma be, bu tu má gise.

ref: But I tell you that Solomon with all his wealth wasn't as well clothed as one of them.
text: Ama, m suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, haari Fɛmɛ Solomon, ni i neŋge n kɛrɛ bo i yo la i ni, wɔ la má sutura bo i yo nyumi juuri nyini wura pum ni be.

ref: God gives such beauty to everything that grows in the fields, even though it is here today and thrown into a fire tomorrow. God will surely do even more for you! Why do you have such little faith?
text: Nzɛn wuram bo bu wo bɛrɛ nyuma, na ahunma a ŋú ka ba kpiɛ bu na bu yara bu, ye Nyɛmɛ niɛ bu dika, nzu ti ye i níɛ má ambɛrɛ bo am tara wuram co n a? Ambɛrɛ, am yarada n ti kaan sɔ.

ref: Only people who don't know God are always worrying about such things. Your Father in heaven knows you need all of these.
text: Dama bo bu su má Nyɛmɛ ni, bɛrɛ sunsun sɔ saŋga kɛrɛ a. Am Si Nyɛmɛ si ka am koro nyinim n kɛrɛ.

ref: God will be as hard on you as you are on others! He will treat you exactly as you treat them.
text: Dama kabo am jɔjɔ am beŋgum jɔrɛ jaraki bu ni, sɔ ye Nyɛmɛ gusu jɔjɔ am gusu jɔrɛ jaraki am a. Gɔŋgɔ bo am fa susu ma minɛm ni, sɔ ye Nyɛmɛ gusu fá susu ma am a.

ref: You can see the speck in your friend's eye, but you don't notice the log in your own eye.
text: Nzu ti ye a niɛ ŋu wura mbaa n bo i wo u niwa nyu­mbaa nu, ye a niɛ ŋu má yeenzin n bo i wo u dɛɛ nyu­mbaa n nu a?

ref: How can you say, “My friend, let me take the speck out of your eye,” when you don't see the log in your own eye?
text: A yo sɛ koro se u niwa ka, ‘Ma m yi wura mbaa n bo i wo u nyu­mbaa nu ni,’ ye saŋga kɛrɛ yeenzin n wo u dɛɛ nyu­mbaa n nu a?

ref: You're nothing but show-offs! First, take the log out of your own eye; then you can see how to take the speck out of your friend's eye.
text: Wɔrɔ burukɔɔ­nu­fɔ ni, du mɔ yi yeenzin n bo i wo u nyu­mbaa nu n ka, na a nyá ŋu asi nyunyumi na a yi wura mbaa n bo i wo u niwa n dɛɛ nyu­mbaa nu ni.

ref: As bad as you are, you still know how to give good gifts to your children. But your heavenly Father is even more ready to give good things to people who ask.
text: Tɔ, ambɛrɛ, haari ni bo am ti satiɛ­yo­fɔm ni, am si aceeri kpam bo am fá ce am nbaam, nzu ti ye nzɛn am sereeri am Si Nyɛmɛ bo i wo aŋgoro ni, i koro ma má am aceeri kpam a?

ref: Would any of you give your hungry child a stone, if the child asked for some bread?
text: Ambɛrɛ nu, ŋma ye i wa sere i diire, ye i mi kéte na i fá yabuɛ ma i wa n a? Ai, be yo má sɔ.

ref: Everyone who asks will receive. Everyone who searches will find. And the door will be opened for everyone who knocks.
text: Dama sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i sere Nyɛmɛ deke be, i má i, na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i niɛniɛ deke be, i ŋú i, na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i bobo anɔ ni, i téke i ma i.

ref: Would you give your child a snake if the child asked for a fish?
text: Wara, ambɛrɛ nu, ŋma ye i wa sere i jombaa ye i kéte na i fá woo ma i wa n a? Ai, be yo má sɔ.

ref: Treat others as you want them to treat you. This is what the Law and the Prophets are all about. The Narrow Gate (Luke
text: Nyini ti, kabo am koro ka minɛm yó ma am ni, am yo i sɔ ma bu, dama Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n jɔrɛ n kɛrɛ lɛɛ, na i nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm dɛɛ buka su.” Kabo Yesu jɔjɔ fa kɔɔri anɔ n bo i ti fii n jɔrɛ ni

ref: But the gate to life is very narrow. The road that leads there is so hard to follow that only a few people find it. A Tree and Its Fruit (Luke
text: Ama, atin n bo i fa minɛm kɔ ŋgɔɔ woo­biri ni, i anɔ n ti fii wɔ, na i atin gusu nati ti kekereke, nyini dɛɛ ti, minɛm bo bu ŋu nyini atin n na bu su i su ni, bu sunnu má.” Kabo Yesu ŋa ya fa minɛm sa­yoo­wa fa si bu ni

ref: Watch out for false prophets! They dress up like sheep, but inside they are wolves who have come to attack you.
text: Na Yesu akaaki ase ka, “Am si am ŋu sa ni kɔrɛ­fɔm bo bu yo bu ŋu ka bu ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm ni. Bu yo bu ŋu bɛnlɛ ka nbɔɛm wɔ, ama, bu ti ka bedim wɔ, na bu ti tiɛ.

ref: You can tell what they are by what they do. No one picks grapes or figs from thornbushes.
text: Bu sa­yoo­wa n nu, ye am koro fa si kabo bu ti barasu a. Am aŋu ka buwe asu koto? Wara, am aŋu ka bedikantoŋga asu ndɔrɔwa? Ai.

ref: A good tree produces good fruit, and a bad tree produces bad fruit.
text: Ama, i ti sɔ ɔ lɛɛ, ka baka kpa kɛrɛ su suu­wa kpa a, na baka ŋgbɛɛn kɛrɛ su suu­wa ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn.

ref: A good tree cannot produce bad fruit, and a bad tree cannot produce good fruit.
text: Baka kpa koro su má suu­wa ŋgbɛɛn, na baka ŋgbɛɛn gusu koro su má suu­wa kpa.

ref: Every tree producing bad fruit will be chopped down and burned.
text: Baka kɛrɛ bo i su má suu­wa kpa, bu kpiɛ i wɔ, na bu fa ji sin nu.

ref: Anyone who hears my teachings and doesn't obey them is like a foolish person who built a house on sand.
text: Ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i tiiri m dɛɛ nɔaniɛm n na wɔ de má i, afa ayo má juma, i mi ti ka sinnzin be bo i taari i awuru ŋmiɛ nu n wɔ.

ref: Rain poured down, rivers flooded, and the winds blew and beat against that house. Finally, it fell with a crash.
text: Na nzue aba atɔ, na wawa n kɛrɛ ayi asandi, na aŋuma akpisi awuru n na i kɛrɛ abereki atɔ liim. Nyini awuru n tɔɔ­wa n tiiri tiɛ haari.”

ref: Not everyone who calls me their Lord will get into the kingdom of heaven. Only the ones who obey my Father in heaven will get in.
text: Na Yesu akaaki ase ka, “Má sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i bo fere m ka, ‘M Mibiɛ, m Mibiɛ,’ ni wura Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n nu a, se má ka bo bu yo m Si Nyɛmɛ bo i wo aŋgoro n dɛɛ koroo­wa ni.

ref: On the day of judgment many will call me their Lord. They will say, “We preached in your name, and in your name we forced out demons and worked many miracles.”
text: Jina cɛɛn n ba juuri, minɛ dɔŋgu bá se m ka, ‘Ya Mibiɛ, niɛ e. Ya yo faari u dɛɛ duma, na ya fa akan Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ, na ya fa u duma n afɔn ayi ajinim afite minɛm ŋu, na ya kaaki afa u duma n ayo alekutura sam dɔŋgu.’

ref: But I will tell them, “I will have nothing to do with you! Get out of my sight, you evil people!” Two Builders (Luke
text: Na m nya se bu ka, ‘Ambɛrɛ satiɛ­yo­fɔm ni, am fa cinsi m bi bɛrɛ. M si má am cɛɛn n be cɛɛn n be da.’” Miɛɛnu fa kɔ ataari ta­fɔ nnyɔ nbem jɔrɛ

ref: Rain poured down, rivers flooded, and winds beat against that house. But it was built on solid rock, and so it did not fall.
text: Na nzue aba atɔ, na wawa n kɛrɛ ayi asandi, na aŋuma akpisi awuru ni, ama, wɔ tɔ má, dama i jaa jina yabuɛ su n dɛɛ ti.

ref: When Jesus finished speaking, the crowds were surprised at his teaching.
text: Saŋga bo Yesu ba jɔjɔɔri sɔ wieeri n na jama n ayo alekutura fa kɔ i keree­wa n su,

ref: He taught them like someone with authority, and not like their teachers of the Law of Moses.
text: dama i keree­wa n awo i ŋgumi ni Yuda­fɔm dɛɛ Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm ni. I keree­wa n nu la yiko.

ref: But the ones who should have been in the kingdom will be thrown out into the dark. They will cry and grit their teeth in pain.”
text: Ama, na minɛm n bo má i ti ye ni ka bɛrɛ wo Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n nu ni, na Nyɛmɛ fa bu tu awosin nu. Dɔ na bu sún, na bu kán bu saa.”

ref: That evening many people with demons in them were brought to Jesus. And with only a word he forced out the evil spirits and healed everyone who was sick.
text: Nyini cɛɛn n naasɔ, na ba fa minɛm beberebe n bo ajinim wo bu ŋu ni, aba Yesu dɔ. Na wɔ ma ajinim nɔɔ na ba fite, na wɔ tuntun tukpaki­fɔm na bu kɛrɛ anya laifiɛ.

ref: Suddenly a man with leprosy came and knelt in front of Jesus. He said, “Lord, you have the power to make me well, if only you wanted to.”
text: Na koko­fɔ be aba i dɔ, na wɔ ba akutu i nyunu asere i ka i buka i, i ŋa, “M Mibiɛ, nzɛn a koro ka a ma m nya laifiɛ, a koro ma m nya i.”

ref: Jesus put his hand on the man and said, “I want to! Now you are well.” At once the man's leprosy disappeared.
text: Na Yesu ateŋge i saa anana i ŋu, na wɔ se i ka, “M koro. Nya laifiɛ.” Na kokobi n akaba ayaki sɔnɔ n na wɔ nya laifiɛ.

ref: “I will go and heal him,” Jesus replied.
text: Na Yesu ase i ka, “M bá na m ma i nya laifiɛ.”

ref: When Jesus heard this, he was so surprised that he turned and said to the crowd following him, “I tell you in all of Israel I've never found anyone with this much faith!
text: Saŋga bo Yesu tiiri sɔ ni, na wɔ yo alekutura, na wɔ se minɛm bo bu su i su n ka, “Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am n a, ma nya aŋu má sɔnɔ bo i la yarada agaya ka ahin sɔnɔ n ŋusu, haari fa saŋga ni Yisareli­fɔm mmɔɔ.

ref: “Lord, my servant is at home in such terrible pain that he can't even move.”

text: Nbem gusu de Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n kabo alɛ­mbaa n tɔtɔɔri lɔlɔɛ nu n wɔ. ref: The Pharisees and the teachers of the Law are experts in the Law of Moses.
text: “Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm, ni Farasi­fɔm n ade Moyisi dɛɛ jinaa­biri ni, ye bu kere minɛm Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n a.

ref: So obey everything they teach you, but don't do as they do. After all, they say one thing and do something else.
text: Nyini ti, deke kɛrɛ bo bu fa kere am ni, am soro i su, ama, má am yo sam bo bu yo bu ni, dama bɛrɛ yo má sam bo bu kere ka minɛm yo bu ni.

ref: They pile heavy burdens on people's shoulders and won't lift a finger to help.
text: Bu cici sɔɔri nɔnɔɔri sɔ minɛm wɔ, ama, bɛrɛ ni bu tii soro má su ka bu fa bu saa kan i.

ref: Everything they do is just to show off in front of others. They even make a big show of wearing Scripture verses on their foreheads and arms, and they wear big tassels for everyone to see.
text: Deke kɛrɛ bo bu yo i, bu koro ka minɛm ŋu bu wɔ, na bu kansi bu. Bu kɛrɛ Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n wɔ, na bu yo i ka sɛbɛ, na bu fa cici bu ŋuma su, adena minɛm ŋu i. Na bu yo bu tanlɛɛ nɔɔ njɔrɛm tintiin.

ref: And when they are in the market, they like to have people greet them as their teachers.
text: Bu koro ka minɛm suyo bu ansɛ gɔɔsu wɔ, na bu ma bu jirima, na bu fere bu ka, ‘Ya Kere­fɔ.’

ref: But none of you should be called a teacher. You have only one teacher, and all of you are like brothers and sisters.
text: Ambɛrɛ, má am ma be fere am ka ‘Ya Kere­fɔ,’ dama am la kere­fɔ kun n kpein wɔ. Ambɛrɛ kɛrɛ ti nibaam wɔ.

ref: Don't call anyone on earth your father. All of you have the same Father in heaven.
text: Má am fere be ka am si, durunya n nu fɛn, dama am la Si kun n kpein wɔ, yiri wo aŋgoro n a.

ref: None of you should be called the leader. The Messiah is your only leader.
text: Má am ma be fere am ka i kpiɛn, dama am kpiɛn ti kun n kpein wɔ, yiri la Kristo Tii­de­fɔ ni.

ref: You Pharisees and teachers of the Law of Moses are in for trouble! You're nothing but show-offs. You lock people out of the kingdom of heaven. You won't go in yourselves, and you keep others from going in.
text: Ambɛrɛ Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm ni Farasi­fɔm n bo am ti burukɔɔ­nu­fɔm ni, am bá ŋu anyum­boro. Am sisi nkuna­fɔm na am fin bu neŋgem wɔ, na am jina sere Nyɛmɛ cɛ, adena minɛm ŋu ka am ti minɛ kpam wɔ. Jina cɛɛn, ambɛrɛ bá nya sui­cin tara minɛm kɛrɛ.]

ref: You Pharisees and teachers of the Law of Moses are in for trouble! You're nothing but show-offs. You travel over land and sea to win one follower. And when you have done so, you make that person twice as fit for hell as you are.
text: Ambɛrɛ Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm ni Farasi­fɔm n bo am ti burukɔɔ­nu­fɔm ni, am bá ŋu anyum­boro. Am kpiɛ tiekum wɔ, na am kɔ miɛm bo bu waarim su, adena am nya sɔnɔ kun mmɔɔ bo i káaki i wo na i su am su, na saŋga bo am nya nyaari i mi, am ma i satiɛ yoo­wa tara am dɛɛ wɔ, adena yiri dú am mɔ kɔ cɛɛma sin nu.

ref: You are in for trouble! You are supposed to lead others, but you are blind. You teach that it doesn't matter if a person swears by the temple. But you say it does matter if someone swears by the gold in the temple.
text: Ambɛrɛ bo am ti ka anyinsinm na am kere minɛm atin ni, am bá ŋu anyum­boro. Am ŋa nzɛn sɔnɔ taanri ndiɛ fa kɔ Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n dɔ, i ti má pui. Ama, nzɛn sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i taanri sika neŋgem n bo bu wo Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu ni, i mi ma i ndiɛ taan­wa n yo yii­wa.

ref: You blind fools! Which is greater, the gold or the temple that makes the gold sacred?
text: Ambɛrɛ bo am ti ka anyinsin sinnzinm ni, bɔnɔ tara i beŋgu a? Sika neŋgem ni, nzɛn Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n bo yiri ma sika neŋgem n yo casi n a?

ref: You also teach that it doesn't matter if a person swears by the altar. But you say it does matter if someone swears by the gift on the altar.
text: Am kaaki ŋa, nzɛn sɔnɔ taanri Mikun n yoo­wa yoo­biri ni, i ti má pui. Ama, nzɛn sɔnɔ taanri aceerim bo ba fa ase Mikun n yoo­wa yoo­biri n su ni, wɔ yo daka ka i mi ma i ndiɛ taan­wa n yo yii­wa.

ref: Are you blind? Which is more important, the gift or the altar that makes the gift sacred?
text: Ambɛrɛ bo am ti ka anyinsinm ni, bɔnɔ tara i beŋgu a? Aceerim ni, nzɛn Mikun n yoo­wa yoo­biri n bo i ma aceerim n yo casi n a?

ref: And anyone who swears by the temple also swears by God, who lives there.
text: Na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i taanri Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru ni, i mi atan i ni Nyɛmɛ bo i wo nu n wɔ lɛɛ.

ref: To swear by heaven is the same as swearing by God's throne and by the one who sits on that throne.
text: Na nzɛn sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i taanri aŋgoro ni, i mi atan Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya biɛ n wɔ lɛɛ, ni yiri Nyɛmɛ bo i tana su ni.

ref: You Pharisees and teachers are show-offs, and you're in for trouble! You give God a tenth of the spices from your garden, such as mint, dill, and cumin. Yet you neglect the more important matters of the Law, such as justice, mercy, and faithfulness. These are the important things you should have done, though you should not have left the others undone either.
text: Ambɛrɛ Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm, ni Farasi­fɔm n bo am ti burukɔɔ­nu­fɔm ni, am bá ŋu anyum­boro. Am yi am jakam ma Nyɛmɛ wɔ, haari ni am maakunm, ni ŋgɛtɛm, ni nvan­nvanm kɛrɛ, ama, am yo má sam bo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n kere ka am yo bu n na bu la anyunuyi tara bu kɛrɛ ni. Bɛrɛ la nahɔrɛ sam, ni aŋunfɔ si, ni yarada­yo. Wɔ yo daka ka na am yo nyini sam saŋga kɛrɛ, na am fa jaka yii­wam buka su.

ref: You Pharisees and teachers are show-offs, and you're in for trouble! You wash the outside of your cups and dishes, while inside there is nothing but greed and selfishness.
text: Ambɛrɛ Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm, ni Farasi­fɔm n bo am ti burukɔɔ­nu­fɔm ni, am bá ŋu anyum­boro. Am ŋmisi am kɔpum ni taalɛm sin wɔ, ama, na bu kunnu ayi ni dekem bo am fin bu faŋga, ni anyii sa­yoo­wam wɔ.

ref: You blind Pharisee! First clean the inside of a cup, and then the outside will also be clean.
text: Wɔrɔ Farasi­fɔ bo a ti ka anyinsin ni, du mɔ yo u kɔpu n kunnu casi ka, na i sin n gusu nyá yo casi.

ref: You Pharisees and teachers are in for trouble! You're nothing but show-offs. You're like tombs that have been whitewashed. On the outside they are beautiful, but inside they are full of bones and filth.
text: Ambɛrɛ Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm, ni Farasi­fɔm n bo am ti burukɔɔ­nu­fɔm ni, am bá ŋu anyum­boro. Am ti ka faŋ­kunmam bo ba bo bu nzue fofoe kpakpari, na bu yo nyunyumi n wɔ, ama, bu kunnu yiri ayi ni fuin buwem wɔ, ni fin barasu kɛrɛ.

ref: You Pharisees and teachers are nothing but show-offs, and you're in for trouble! You build monuments for the prophets and decorate the tombs of good people.
text: Na Yesu asa su ase ka, “Ambɛrɛ Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm, ni Farasi­fɔm n bo am ti burukɔɔ­nu­fɔm ni, am bá ŋu anyum­boro. Am yo faŋ­kunmam dɔŋgu fa ma Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm wɔ, na am yo minɛm bo bu yo sa bo i kɔ atin su Nyɛmɛ dɔ n faŋ­kunmam nyunyumi.

ref: And you claim you would not have taken part with your ancestors in killing the prophets.
text: Na am se ka, nzɛn má ambɛrɛ yo woori bɛrɛ, am nam saŋga ni, má am kun má Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm n ni, kabo am nam yo yoori bu ni.

ref: But you prove you really are the relatives of the ones who killed the prophets.
text: Nyini ti, am asoro su ka am ti bɛrɛ bo bu yo kuunri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm n dɛɛ busu nu­fɔm wɔ lɛɛ.

ref: You are nothing but snakes and the children of snakes! How can you escape going to hell?
text: Ambɛrɛ burukɔɔ­nu­fɔm bo am ti woom na am ti ka pumpuni nbaam ni, am yo sɛ ye am koro ŋmati fite nu, bo am wura má cɛɛma sin n nu a?

ref: I can promise that you people living today will be punished for all these things! Jesus Loves Jerusalem (Luke
text: Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am n a, nyini n kɛrɛ dɛɛ ti ye Nyɛmɛ bá cin ambɛrɛ nyuma­fɔm sui n a.” Kabo Yesu ahore saaki maari Yɛrusalem­fɔm barasu ni

ref: You won't see me again until you say, “Blessed is the one who comes in the name of the Lord.”
text: M suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, fite kisa fa kɔ, am ŋú má m kun, má ka saŋga bo am bá se ka, ‘Tere­fiɛ wo yiri n bo i suba Micɛra n dɛɛ duma n nu n dɔ.’”

ref: Many will come and claim to be me. They will say they are the Messiah, and they will fool many people.
text: Dama minɛ dɔŋgu bá fa m duma ba, na bu sé ka, ‘Kristo Tii­de­fɔ n la mini wɔ.’ Na bu dádaka minɛ beberebe.

ref: You will soon hear about wars and threats of wars, but don't be afraid. These things will have to happen first, but that isn't the end.
text: Saŋga bo am bá ti aluwa ŋga fɛn, wara bu sukun luɛ dɔ, má am ma am ahore saaki. Wɔ yo daka ka nyini sam n kɛrɛ bá yo sin ka, ama, má am niɛ ka durunya n awieeri lɛɛ.

ref: Later, as Jesus was sitting on the Mount of Olives, his disciples came to him in private and asked, “When will this happen? What will be the sign of your coming and of the end of the world?”
text: Saŋga bo Yesu fun kɔ tanaari Oliva Buka n su ni, na i susu­fɔm n aba i dɔ, bu ni yiri ŋgumi, na ba se i ka, “Ya Kere­fɔ, se ya, saŋga bɔnɔ ye ahin sam kɛrɛ bá yo a? Ye nzu deke bá kere ya ka saŋga n adodo bo a bá bekun, na durunya n bá awieeri a?”

ref: Nations and kingdoms will go to war against each other. People will starve to death, and in some places there will be earthquakes.
text: Daanfiɛ daanfiɛ bá kun luɛ ni bu beŋgum, na nfɛmɛm kún luɛ ni bu beŋgu nfɛmɛm. Na asiɛn bá kpisi awɔrɛ awɔrɛm nu, na ahɔɛ tɔ́.

ref: It will be a terrible time for women who are expecting babies or nursing young children.
text: Nyini saŋga ni yó kekereke agaya ma nyinsɛ­fɔm, ni nbaram bo bu nbaam toko nu ni.

ref: But this is just the beginning of troubles.
text: Nyini n kɛrɛ bá yo kabo bara kutu cɛɛn bumboori dɛɛ wahara n ti n wɔ.

ref: You will be arrested, punished, and even killed. Because of me, you will be hated by people of all nations.
text: Nyini saŋga, na bu tára am ma yiko­fɔm na bu kére am wuwuruwa, na bu kún am. Mini dɛɛ ti, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ kpɔ́ am.

ref: Many false prophets will come and fool a lot of people.
text: Na kɔrɛ­fɔm dɔŋgu bo bu yo bu ŋu ka bɛrɛ ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm ni bá ba, na bu dádaka minɛm beberebe.

ref: And pray that you won't have to escape in winter or on a Sabbath.
text: Am sere Nyɛmɛ ka má nyini sa n yó buke saŋga, wara Yuda­fɔm Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn ni.

ref: If you are living in Judea at that time, run to the mountains.
text: Nyini saŋga ni, wɔrɔ kɛrɛ bo a tiiri i ŋga na a wo Yudiya asiɛn n su, ŋmati kɔ bukam n su.

ref: If you are out in the field, don't go back for your coat.
text: Na wɔrɔ kɛrɛ gusu bo a wo fiesu, má a kpie u nyi kɔ awuru kɔ fa u sutura be.

ref: If God doesn't make the time shorter, no one will be left alive. But because of God's chosen ones, he will make the time shorter.
text: Ama, Nyɛmɛ akpiɛ nyini saŋga n su. Nzɛn má wɔ kpiɛ má i su, má sɔnɔ wo má bɛrɛ bo i nya ŋgɔɔ, ama, i dɛɛ tiɛŋgu minɛm bo wɔ yi bu n dɛɛ ti, ye wɔ kpiɛ saŋga n su a.

ref: When the Son of Man appears, things will be just as they were when Noah lived.
text: Mini Adamande Wa n ba saŋga ni yó kabo sɔnɔ be bo bu fere i ka Nowa saŋga na i yoori n wɔ.

ref: People were eating, drinking, and getting married right up to the day the flood came and Noah went into the big boat.
text: Saŋga bo wɔmbu n nya di má durunya n ni, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ woori di ni nu su wɔ, na nbaram ni nbiɛsɔm suja bu beŋgum, fa kɔ juuri haari cɛɛn n bo Nowa wuraari lee bambaka n nu ni.

ref: They didn't know anything was happening until the flood came and swept them all away. This is how it will be when the Son of Man appears.
text: Minɛm ayo asi má sa bo i bá yo ni, haari cɛɛn n bo wɔmbu n ba diiri bu kɛrɛ ni. I yó sɔ ɔ, fa kɔ saŋga n bo mini Adamande Wa ni bá ba ni.

ref: Two men will be in the same field, but only one will be taken. The other will be left.
text: Nyini saŋga ni, minɛ nnyɔ bá wo juma yo su fiesu, na Nyɛmɛ má bu bá fa kun n kɔ yaki kun ni.

ref: Two women will be together grinding grain, but only one will be taken. The other will be left.
text: Na nbaram nnyɔ wo ŋgani saan­biri, na Nyɛmɛ má bu bá fa kun n kɔ yaki kun ni.

ref: So be on your guard! You don't know when your Lord will come.
text: Tɔ, nyini ti, má am fa am ŋu ji na am daafi, dama am si má cɛɛn n bo am Mibiɛ ni bá ni.

ref: Homeowners never know when a thief is coming, and they are always on guard to keep one from breaking in.
text: Am si tenle su ka nzɛn má awuru­fɔ­biɛ n si saŋga bo awiefɔ ni bá i dika na i bá wɔ i, má i tana ŋminda a, na awiefɔ n koro ba wura má.

ref: Always be ready! You don't know when the Son of Man will come. Faithful and Unfaithful Servants (Luke
text: Nyini ti, ambɛrɛ gusu kpa am ŋu saŋga kɛrɛ, dama mini Adamande Wa ni, m bá saŋga bo am sunsun má ka m bá n wɔ.” Miɛɛnu fa kɔ akɔɔ nbem dabuŋu n jɔrɛ

ref: But suppose one of the servants thinks the master won't return until late.
text: Ama, nzɛn i ti akɔɔ tiɛ­fɔ, i súnsun ka i mibiɛ ni kpíe má i nyi nde.

ref: Who are faithful and wise servants? Who are the ones the master will put in charge of giving the other servants their food supplies at the proper time?
text: Na Yesu akaaki ase ka, “Akɔɔ kpiɛn n bo i la dabuŋu na i kaaki la alecira n la ŋma a? Yiri la bo i mibiɛ n afa akɔɔ nyiŋgam awura i saa nu ka i kpaaki diire nu ma bu, saŋga bo i ti ye ni ka i ma bu ni.

ref: Servants are fortunate if their master comes and finds them doing their job.
text: Nzɛn i mibiɛ n ba ŋuuri ka i suyo i juma kabo i seeri ka i yo ni, tere­fiɛ wo nyini akɔɔ n dɔ.

ref: You may be sure a servant who is always faithful will be put in charge of everything the master owns.
text: Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am n a, mibiɛ ni fá i aja kɛrɛ wura nyini akɔɔ n saa nu wɔ, na i níɛ i dika.

ref: Suppose this evil servant starts beating the other servants and eats and drinks with people who are drunk.
text: A ŋu ka i subo akɔɔ nyiŋgam wɔ, na i sudi, na i ni nzan nu­fɔm sunu bo.

ref: If that happens, the master will surely come on a day and at a time when the servant least expects him.
text: Dɔ, na i mibiɛ n ba kpieeri i nyi baari cɛɛn bo i sunsun má ka i bá, na i kaaki si má ba n saŋga ni.

ref: This servant will then be punished and thrown out with the ones who only pretended to serve their master. There they will cry and grit their teeth in pain.
text: Nyini saŋga n na i mibiɛ n cín i sui agaya, na i fá i bo burukɔɔ­nu­fɔm n su. Dɔ na i sún, na i kán i saa.”

ref: The foolish ones took their lamps, but no extra oil.
text: Na sinnzinm n afa bu fitanam, ama, ba bita má ŋguin nu,

ref: The ones who were wise took along extra oil for their lamps.
text: na alecira­fɔm n bɛrɛ afa ŋguin abita nu.

ref: Then in the middle of the night someone shouted, “Here's the groom! Come to meet him!”
text: Na jinanjin saŋga, na ba ti ka bu subo ndoori ka, ‘Konyafan ni suba o, am fite na ya kɔ kpa i atin.’

ref: When the women got up and started getting their lamps ready,
text: Na nsuŋguru burum kɛrɛ ayasu, ayo bu fitanam dɔŋgu.

ref: the foolish ones said to the others, “Let us have some of your oil! Our lamps are going out.”
text: Na sinnzinm n ase alecira­fɔm ni ka, ‘Am bu am ŋguin n be ma ya, ya fitanam ni sunɔ.’

ref: Those who were wise answered, “There's not enough oil for all of us! Go and buy some for yourselves.”
text: Na alecira­fɔm n ase bu ka, ‘Ai, ya ŋguin ni koro ju má ya ni ambɛrɛ kɛrɛ. Am kɔ ŋguin tɔ­fɔm dɔ, na am kɔ to.’

ref: So, my disciples, always be ready! You don't know the day or the time when all this will happen. A Story about Three Servants (Luke
text: Na Yesu ase i susu­fɔm n ka, “Tɔ, nyini ti, má am fa am ŋu ji na am daafi, dama am si má cɛɛn n bo m bá ni.” Miɛɛnu fa kɔ akɔɔ nsan nbem dabuŋu n jɔrɛ

ref: Some time later the master of those servants returned. He called them in and asked what they had done with his money.
text: Na bu mibiɛ n akɔ acɛ dɔ, haari na wɔ ba aba ka i ba niɛ kabo bu bita i ŋwaa n barasu ni.

ref: The kingdom is also like what happened when a man went away and put his three servants in charge of all he owned.
text: Na Yesu ase bu ka, “Saŋga bo m bá ba ni, i yó kabo sɔnɔ be sukɔ atin, na i fereeri i akɔɔm, na i faari i neŋge kɛrɛ wuraari bu saa nu, ka bu niɛ i dika n wɔ.

ref: As soon as the man had gone, the servant with the
text: Na bo i maari i busu nnu ni afa abo wata bu, na wɔ nya i ŋusu nnu abuka su.

ref: “Wonderful!” his master replied. “You are a good and faithful servant. I left you in charge of only a little, but now I will put you in charge of much more. Come and share in my happiness!”
text: Na i mibiɛ n ase i ka, ‘Nfan. Wɔrɔ akɔɔ kpa bo a la dabuŋu ni, kabo a kere ka a la dabuŋu niɛ deke kaan su ni, m fá dɔŋgu n mmɔɔ wura u saa nu na a niɛ i dika. Bra na a ba nya ahorejɔ n bo ma nya i n be.’

ref: “Wonderful!” his master replied. “You are a good and faithful servant. I left you in charge of only a little, but now I will put you in charge of much more. Come and share in my happiness!”
text: Na i mibiɛ n ase i ka, ‘Nfan. Wɔrɔ akɔɔ kpa bo a la dabuŋu ni, kabo a kere ka a la dabuŋu niɛ deke kaan su ni, m fá dɔŋgu n mmɔɔ wura u saa nu na a niɛ i dika. Bra na a ba nya ahorejɔ n bo ma nya i n be.’

ref: I was frightened and went out and hid your money in the ground. Here is every single coin!”
text: Nyini ti ye sɛrɛ tiiri m a, na ma fundu kunma afa u ŋwaa n afiɛ i nu. Yiri yaa, de u deke.’

ref: The master of the servant told him, “You are lazy and good-for-nothing! You know I harvest what I don't plant and gather crops where I haven't scattered seed.
text: Na i mibiɛ n akaaki ase i ka, ‘Wɔrɔ akɔɔ tiɛ nviɛya­fɔ ni, a si tenle su ka deke bo ma dɔ má i ni, m kpiɛ nyini wɔ, na deke bo ma bobo má i ni, m tiɛntiɛn nyini nɔɔ a.

ref: Then the master said, “Now your money will be taken away and given to the servant with
text: Dɔ na mibiɛ n ase i minɛm ka, ‘Tɔ, am de ŋwaa ni i dɔ kisa kisa, na am fa ma akɔɔ n bo i bita ŋwaa n busu buru ni.

ref: Everyone who has something will be given more, and they will have more than enough. But everything will be taken from those who don't have anything.
text: Dama sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo wɔ nya deke na i mi si i bita, bu má i buka su wɔ, na i nyá beberebe, ama, bo i si má i bita, haari ni nyini kaan n bo i la i ni, bu cín de i dɔ ɔ.

ref: You are a worthless servant, and you will be thrown out into the dark where people will cry and grit their teeth in pain.” The Final Judgment
text: Ama, m ahin akɔɔ ŋgbɛɛn ni, am fa i tu awosin n nu, deke bo i sún, na i kán i saa.’” Kabo Yesu bá di minɛm jɔrɛ barasu ni

ref: When the Son of Man comes in his glory with all his angels, he will sit on his royal throne.
text: Na Yesu akaaki ase ka, “Saŋga bo mini Adamande Wa ni bá baari ni m jirima woo­wa n nu ni, ni Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛm kɛrɛ ni, m bá tana m jirima fɛmɛya biɛ n su.

ref: The people of all nations will be brought before him, and he will separate them, as shepherds separate their sheep from their goats.
text: Na durunya n nu minɛm kɛrɛ bá tiɛn bu nɔɔ m nyunu, na m kpáaki bu nu, busu nnyɔ, kabo bɔɛ­sasa­fɔ kpaaki yi nbɔɛm ni sirim nu barasu ni.

ref: Then the king will say to those on his right, “My father has blessed you! Come and receive the kingdom that was prepared for you before the world was created.
text: Na mini fɛmɛ n sé bo bu wo m saa fɔmbɔrɔ su n ka, ‘Ambɛrɛ bo m Si Nyɛmɛ ayo am kisi ni, am bra na am ba de fɛmɛya n bo wɔ sɛsɛ i su ase ama am, fite durunya n bumboori kɛrɛ ni.

ref: When I was hungry, you gave me something to eat, and when I was thirsty, you gave me something to drink. When I was a stranger, you welcomed me,

ref: Jesus asked them, “What does the Law of Moses say about this?”
text: Na Yesu abɔ bu nu ka, “Nɔaniɛ maa­wa bɔnɔ ye Moyisi yo fa maari am a?”

ref: Jesus replied, “Moses gave you this law because you are so heartless.
text: Na Yesu ase bu ka, “Am ahore­mbaa kekereke n dɛɛ ti ye Moyisi yo kɛrɛɛri nyini nɔaniɛ maa­wa n maari am a.

ref: But in the beginning God made a man and a woman.
text: Ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka fite durunya n bumboori, ‘Nyɛmɛ yiiri biɛsɔ ni bara a.’
